data.title: Amazon Shares Slip on Reports of 16,000 Job Cuts
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN, Financials) shares edged lower after reports surfaced that the company will cut roughly 16,000 jobs this week as part of a broader restructuring effort. The layoffs will mostly affect corporate workers, especially those who work in India. They make up approximately 1% of Amazon's 1.57 million employees worldwide.

The layoffs are the biggest Amazon has made since 2023, when the company lost 27,000 people as part of a massive cost cutting effort. Amazon is trying to cut costs and make things run more smoothly since demand for its core e commerce and cloud services is slowing down.

Sources say that more layoffs could happen, which could bring the total number of jobs down to as many as 30,000 by the middle of 2026. Amazon's cloud and AI businesses are still growing, but the company is having trouble with its profit margins since worldwide retail sales are slowing down and infrastructure expenses are going up.

On Monday, Amazon's stock fell by 0.4%, although it is still up more than 3% this year. Wall Street analysts still give the stock a "Strong Buy" rating, with an average price target of $295, which means that investors might make 23% more money.

data.title: HBT Financial slides as quarterly net income falls
data.description:
** Shares of lender HBT Financial fall 2.90% to $26.77

** HBT reports Q4 net income of $18.94 million, or 60 cents per share, in the three months ended December 31, compared with $20.27 million, or 64 cents per share, a year earlier

** Loans increased by $56.2 million, driven by new originations in construction, land development and multi-family segments

** Deposits rose by $12.1 million, despite moving $50 million off balance sheet due to strong liquidity

** Co anticipates merger with CNB Bank to close in Q1 2026

** Including session's move, HBT up 15.8% in the last 12 months

data.title: Silver Rally Ratchets Up on Dollar Weakness — Market Talk
data.description: Silver jumped today, taking strength from a weakening U.S. dollar index. Both silver and gold climbed to fresh highs, while the USD slid 0.6% ahead of the Federal Reserve's rate decision Wednesday. A lower dollar means the appetite for other value-holding assets increases, says Antonio Di Giacomo of XS.com in a note. "The dollar index remains near its lowest levels since September 2025... which increases the purchasing power of international investors and boosts global demand for the precious metal," says Di Giacomo. Front-month silver closes up 14% to $115.08 a troy ounce, a new high and the largest percentage gain since March 1985. Gold finishes up 2.1% to a new high of $5,079.70/oz. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Orezone Gold to Buy Quebec Mine From Hecla Mining With Financing From Franco-Nevada — Update
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton and Adriano Marchese

Hecla Mining agreed to sell a Quebec gold mine to Orezone Gold, as prices for the metal continue to rally and on Monday broke past a US$5,000 spot price for the first time.

Orezone on Monday said it entered into a definitive agreement to buy Hecla Quebec, which houses the Casa Berardi gold mine as well as a portfolio of exploration properties in the province, for up to US$593 million in total consideration. Orezone said it lined up a US$100 million streaming agreement with Franco-Nevada to help finance the deal.

The acquisition consists of an upfront consideration of US$160 million in cash and US$112 million in Orezone shares, representing 9.9% of the pro-forma issued and outstanding shares of the company.

There would also be two cash payments of US$80 million over the course of about three years and contingent consideration of up to US$241 million comprising royalty payments, permit-receipt payments and gold-price-linked payments.

"This transaction marks a significant inflection point for Orezone as it adds a proven, cash-flow-generating asset to our portfolio," Patrick Downey, Orezone's chief executive officer, said, adding that Casa Berardi's history and potential would provide a foundation for sustained growth.

As part of its financing deal with the Vancouver, British Columbia, company, Franco-Nevada would receive 1,625 ounces of gold every quarter for the first five years. After that, it would get a share of production, which would include 5% of the gold from the Casa Berardi mine and other Quebec assets, and 2.5% of the gold from the Heva-Hosco project.

The deal would bring in a gold supply from the long-running operation in a well-known mining region, Franco-Nevada said. The Casa Berardi mine, in Quebec's Abitibi district, has been producing gold for about 35 years through both open pit and underground work, yielding more than 3.2 million ounces so far and remains a steady source of output and cash flow, the Toronto company added.

"We are delighted to partner with Orezone in this transformative transaction to acquire the Casa Berardi mine and portfolio of exploration properties in Quebec," Franco-Nevada CEO Paul Brink said. "Patrick and the Orezone team have an excellent track record operating and developing mines," he added.

Hecla, which is based in Coeur D'Alene, Idaho, said the transaction is part of its broader goal to focus on silver assets. The deal is slated to close in the first quarter of 2026.

The company said earlier on Monday it expects lower gold production in 2026, in part due to lower milled grades at the Greens Creek and Casa Berardi mines.

Shares of Orezone are up about 17%, at 2.80 Canadian dollars (US$2.04), and Franco-Nevada stock has gained 3%, to C$361.28, in Toronto. Hecla shares have slipped 0.6%, to US$31.61, in New York.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com and Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Rimini Street to Report Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results on February 19, 2026
data.description:
LAS VEGAS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--January 26, 2026--

Rimini Street, Inc. , the Software Support and Agentic AI ERP Company(TM), and the leading third-party support provider for Oracle, SAP and VMware software, today announced it will report earnings after market close on February 19, 2026. The company will host a conference call and webcast on that date to discuss the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025 results and the 2026 outlook at 5:00 p.m. Eastern / 2:00 p.m. Pacific time.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260126188817/en/

Rimini Street to Report Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results on February 19, 2026

A live webcast of the event will be available on Rimini Street's Investor Relations site via the Rimini Street IR events link and directly via the webcast link. Dial-in participants can access the conference by dialing 1-800-836-8184.

A replay of the webcast will be available for one year following the event.

About Rimini Street, Inc.

Rimini Street, Inc. , a Russell 2000(R) Company, is a proven, trusted global provider of end-to-end, mission-critical enterprise software support, managed services and innovative Agentic AI ERP solutions and is the leading third-party support provider for Oracle, SAP and VMware software. The Company has signed thousands of IT service contracts with Fortune Global 100, Fortune 500, midmarket, public sector and government organizations who have leveraged the Rimini Smart Path(TM) methodology to achieve better operational outcomes, billions of US dollars in savings and fund AI and other innovation.

To learn more, please visit www.riministreet.com, and connect with Rimini Street on X, Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn.

Forward-Looking Statements

Certain statements included in this communication are not historical facts but are forward-looking statements for purposes of the safe harbor provisions under The Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Forward-looking statements generally are accompanied by words such as "anticipate," "assume," "believe," "continue," "could," "currently," "estimate," "expect," "forecast," "future," "intend," "may," "might," "outlook," "plan," "possible," "goal," "potential," "predict," "project, " "seem," "seek," "should," "will," "would" or other similar words, phrases or expressions. These forward-looking statements include, but are not limited to, statements regarding our expectations of future events, future opportunities, global expansion and other growth initiatives and our investments in such initiatives. These statements are based on various assumptions and on the current expectations of management and are not predictions of actual performance, nor are these statements of historical facts. These statements are subject to a number of risks and uncertainties regarding Rimini Street's business, and actual results may differ materially. These risks and uncertainties include, but are not limited to, litigation, agreements and Court orders involving Oracle, the wind down of support services for Oracle's PeopleSoft software products and the impact on future period revenue and costs incurred related to these efforts; changes in the business environment in which Rimini Street operates, including the impact of macro-economic trends, geopolitical tensions and changes in foreign exchange rates, as well as general financial, economic, regulatory and political conditions affecting the industry in which we operate and the industries in which our clients operate; the evolution of the enterprise software management and support landscape and our ability to attract and retain clients and further penetrate our client base; significant competition in the software support services industry and our intentions with respect to our pricing model; customer adoption of our expanded portfolio of products and services and products and services we expect to introduce; our expectations regarding new product offerings, partnerships and alliance programs, including but not limited to our partnership with ServiceNow and our Agentic AI ERP innovation solutions; our ability to grow our revenue and accurately forecast revenue, along with the results of any efforts to manage costs to align with revenue expectations and expansion of our offerings; the expected impact of reductions in our workforce during the last and current fiscal year and associated reorganization costs; estimates of our total addressable market and expectations of client savings relative to use of other providers; variability of timing in our sales cycle; risks relating to retention rates, including our ability to accurately predict retention rates; the loss of one or more members of our management team; our ability to attract and retain additional qualified personnel; our business plan and ability to grow in the future and our ability to achieve and maintain profitability; the volatility of our stock price; our need and ability to raise equity or debt financing on favorable terms and our ability to generate cash flows from operations to help fund increased investment in our growth initiatives; risks associated with global operations; our ability to prevent unauthorized access to our information technology systems and other cybersecurity threats; any deficiencies associated with artificial intelligence (AI) technologies used by us or by our third-party vendors and service providers or incorporated by us into our service offerings and/or our Agentic AI ERP innovation solutions; our ability to protect the confidential information of our employees and clients and to comply with privacy regulations; our ability to maintain an effective system of internal control over financial reporting; our ability to maintain, protect and enhance our brand and intellectual property; changes in laws and regulations, including changes in tax laws or unfavorable outcomes of tax positions we take; tariff costs, including tariff relief or the ability to mitigate tariffs, in light of new or increased tariffs imposed by the United States government and the potential for retaliatory trade measures by affected countries; a failure by us to establish adequate tax reserves; adverse developments in and costs associated with defending pending litigation or any new litigation; our ability to realize benefits from our net operating losses; any negative impact of environmental, social and governance matters on our reputation or business and the exposure of our business to additional costs or risks from our reporting on such matters; our ability to maintain our good standing with the United States government and international governments, capture new contracts with governmental entities and maintain our status as an approved United States government contractor; our credit facility's ongoing debt service obligations and financial and operational covenants on our business and related interest rate risk; the sufficiency of our cash and cash equivalents to meet our liquidity requirements; the amount and timing of repurchases, if any, under our stock repurchase program and our ability to enhance stockholder value through such program; uncertainty as to the long-term value of Rimini Street's equity securities; catastrophic events that disrupt our business or that of our clients; and those discussed under the heading "Risk Factors" in Rimini Street's Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q filed on October 30, 2025, and as updated from time to time by Rimini Street's future Annual Reports on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K, and other filings by Rimini Street with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. In addition, forward-looking statements provide Rimini Street's expectations, plans or forecasts of future events and views as of the date of this communication. Rimini Street anticipates that subsequent events and developments will cause Rimini Street's assessments to change. However, while Rimini Street may elect to update these forward-looking statements at some point in the future, Rimini Street specifically disclaims any obligation to do so, except as required by law. data.title: IOVA Shares Jump 8% — What’s Retail Expecting?
data.description:
Key points:
  • On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around IOVA stock stayed within the ‘bullish’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume remained at ‘high’ levels.In the three months through the end of September,
  • Iovance reported total product revenue of $68 million, including about $58 million from Amtagvi sales in the U.S.
  • In November, the company said that it expects full year 2025 revenue of $250 million to $300 million. 

Shares of Iovance Biotherapeutics Inc. (IOVA) garnered retail interest on Monday, as shares climbed up 8% amid anticipation from investors for positive announcements, and takeover hopes.

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around IOVA stock stayed within the ‘bullish’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume remained at ‘high’ levels.

Iovance is a commercial stage immuno-oncology company. It is seeking to deliver tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) therapies for patients with cancer. TIL therapy is a personalized, one-time immunotherapy that extracts a patient's own, naturally occurring T-cells from their tumor, expands them to billions in a lab, and infuses them back to fight advanced cancers.

What Do Stocktwits Users Have To Say?

A Stocktwits user highlighted that the company hasn’t made announcements in the recent weeks. “They've had their head down working at the grindstone. Its time for several updates!” they wrote.

https://www.stocktwits.com/BioCrazy1/message/642913966

Another highlighted takeover opportunities.

https://www.stocktwits.com/beachsidefreddy/message/642912870

Another user opined that IOVA management must have a chat with Merck in light of reports that the pharma giant is no longer in talks to acquire the cancer drug developer Revolution Medicines.

https://www.stocktwits.com/TopCharts/message/642896481

Iovance Pipeline

In the three months through the end of September, Iovance reported total product revenue of $68 million, including about $58 million from Amtagvi sales in the U.S. Amtagvi is FDA-approved for the treatment of metastatic melanoma.

In November, the company said that it expects full year 2025 revenue of $250 million to $300 million. 2025 marks the first full calendar year of Amtagvi sales. Analysts on average expect the company to report total revenue of $259.45 million.

IOVA stock has fallen 54% over the past 12 months.

data.title: AppLovin Stock Is Climbing. The Ad Platform Won Some Big Clients, Analyst Says. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nate Wolf

AppLovin has lost some of its luster since the start of the year, but big wins in the advertising platform's new e-commerce segment could give the stock a boost, according to analysts at Needham & Company.

The firm upgraded AppLovin shares to Buy from Hold and set a $700 price target in a research note Monday, helping to send shares higher. Needham has spotted two major brands — Kalshi and Etsy — using AppLovin's Axon ad-targeting technology in recent months, alongside thousands of other e-commerce advertisers.

AppLovin stock rose 2.1% to $535.33 on Monday after soaring more than 6% in early trading. The stock more than doubled in 2025, but it has fallen 20% so far this year.

Having solidified itself as a leader in mobile gaming ads, AppLovin has been scaling its e-commerce business. Brands can now install the Axon Pixel add-on through Shopify or Alphabet's Google and set up ad campaigns by themselves. The plug-in makes it easier for sell-side analysts to dig through websites to find brands that use Pixel.

Of the more than 2,200 websites Needham searched, 435 brands were using AppLovin as of Jan. 21, up from 431 in late December and 359 last March.

New wins appeared to include the prediction-market platform Kalshi in December and online marketplace Etsy in January, Needham said. That pair joins names like Wayfair, Crocs, Yeti, and e.l.f. Beauty.

Neither Etsy nor Kalshi immediately replied to Barron's requests for confirmation that they are using AppLovin's Axon tool.

Needham now forecasts AppLovin's e-commerce revenue will be $1.45 billion in fiscal 2026, up from a prior estimate of $1.05 billion. That would help push revenue growth to 35.4% in 2026 from an expected 21.8% in 2025.

"APP has already proven itself as a platform for mobile gaming advertisers and we think they are in the process of showing they can be successful outside of games too," wrote analyst Bernie McTernan. "But it is still early days as self-service launched only in late 2025."

AppLovin still has room to outperform Needham's lofty new estimates in the long run, the firm said. The swift ramp up of TikTok in the early 2020s provides a model for AppLovin, since TikTok was the most recent brand-new tool to disrupt performance marketing.

"TikTok was able to achieve significant growth rates even years after launch, which we think represents the bull case for APP," McTernan wrote.

He isn't the only analyst who has made an upbeat call on AppLovin stock in recent weeks. Evercore ISI initiated coverage of the stock with an Outperform rating and an $835 price target earlier this month, citing momentum across the company's gaming and e-commerce businesses.

The e-commerce segment is going up against massive rivals like Google, Meta Platforms, and Amazon.com, but early signs are promising, Evercore's Robert Coolbrith argued.

The platform has "a very large audience and inventory opportunity, attractive demographics, high-impact ad formats, and market-leading machine learning technology," Coolbrith wrote.

Write to Nate Wolf at nate.wolf@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Oak Woods Acquisition Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Oak Woods Acquisition Corp, a blank check company formed for the purpose of entering into a merger or similar business combination, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational status, shedding light on its recent activities and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Net Income: $13,889 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, primarily resulted from interest income of $440,629 on investments held in the Trust Account and an upward change in fair value of warrant liabilities of $1,700, partially offset by formation and operating costs of $425,130.

Net Loss: $134,546 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, which resulted from operating expenses of $1,508,945, partially offset by interest income of $135 on the operating account, interest income of $1,369,264 on investments held in the trust account, and a downward change in fair value of warrant liabilities of $5,000.

Net Income: $335,717 for the three months ended September 30, 2024, which resulted from operating expenses of $473,505 and an upward change in fair value of warrant liabilities of $720, partially offset by interest income of $359 on the operating account and interest income of $809,583 on investments held in the trust account.

Net Loss: $287,416 for the nine months ended September 30, 2024, which resulted from operating expenses of $2,106,888 and an upward change in fair value of warrant liabilities of $400, partially offset by interest income of $5,412 on the operating account and interest income of $2,389,292 on investments held in the trust account.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: The company is a blank check company formed for the purpose of entering into a merger, stock exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, recapitalization, reorganization, or similar business combination with one or more businesses.

Merger Agreement: On August 11, 2023, the company entered into a Merger Agreement with Oak Woods Merger Sub, Inc., Huajin (China) Holdings Limited, and Xuehong Li, with the intention of merging Merger Sub with and into Huajin, making Huajin a wholly-owned subsidiary.

Operational Status: As of September 30, 2025, the company had not commenced any operations except those related to the prospective merger. The company will not generate any operating revenues until after the completion of a business combination.

Recent Developments: The company has been actively extending the time available to complete a business combination through shareholder-approved extensions and deposits into the Trust Account.

Nasdaq Compliance: The company received multiple notifications from Nasdaq regarding non-compliance with listing rules, including the minimum market value of listed securities, minimum public holders requirement, and timely filing of financial reports. The company is working on plans to regain compliance.

Future Outlook: The company intends to use the funds held in the Trust Account to complete an initial business combination and may need to raise additional capital through loans or investments from its Sponsor or other parties to meet working capital needs.

SEC Filing: Oak Woods Acquisition Corp [ OAKU ] - 10-Q - Jan. 26, 2026

data.title: Microsoft Takes Aim at Amazon, Google AI Chips
data.description:
Microsoft just dropped a new AI chip, and the subtext is pretty obvious: it's tired of AI being so expensive.

The chip is called Maia 200, and instead of being some future concept, it's already up and running inside Microsoft's own data centers. That's important. This isn't a promise it's something they're actively using.

At its core, this move is about control. Nvidia still runs the AI chip world, but those chips are hard to get and incredibly pricey. When you're Microsoft and AI demand keeps exploding, that gets uncomfortable fast. So like Amazon and Google , Microsoft is trying to take some pressure off by building more of its own hardware.

Microsoft says Maia 200 is cheaper to run and more efficient than the custom AI chips its cloud rivals use today. In plain terms, that means it can generate AI responses using less power and less money which matters a lot when millions of prompts are flying through Azure every second.

This doesn't mean Microsoft is ditching Nvidia . Not even close. Nvidia is still the gold standard, especially for training big models. But this does show where things are heading. The biggest tech companies don't want to rely on one supplier forever, especially when AI costs are only going up.

data.title: GME Shares Surge Nearly 8% After Michael Burry Goes Long On Stock
data.description:
Key points:
  • Burry also noted CEO Ryan Cohen’s investment in the video game firm, saying he believes in Cohen. 
  • The investor added that he likes the setup, the governance, and the strategy of the firm. 
  • Cohen bought 500,000 shares each on Jan. 20 and Jan. 21, according to filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares of GameStop Corp. (GME) popped nearly 8% on Monday after “The Big Short” investor Michael Burry said he is buying the stock.

"I own GME. I have been buying recently. I expect I am buying at what may soon be 1x tangible book value / 1x net asset value,” said Burry, in a Substack post, as per TheFly.

Burry also noted GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen’s investment in the video game firm, saying he believes in Cohen. “And getting a young Ryan Cohen investing and deploying the company's capital and cash flows. Perhaps for the next 50 years...I am not counting on a short squeeze to realize long-term value,” Burry said.

“I am fifteen years his senior, but not too old to be patient," he added.

The Long-Term Position

Burry is willing to hold the meme stock for the long-term, and is “excited to see where this goes,” he said in the post. The investor added that he likes the setup, the governance, and the strategy of the firm.

“Ryan is making lemonade out of lemons,” Burry said, according to a CNBC report. “He has a crappy business, and he is milking it best he can while taking advantage of the meme stock phenomenon to raise cash and wait for an opportunity to make a big buy of a real growing cash cow business.”

Insider Holdings

Cohen increased his stake in GameStop by buying 500,000 shares on Jan. 20 at an average price of $21.12 per share, according to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Subsequently, Cohen went on to purchase another 500,000 shares on Jan. 21 at a higher average price of $21.60 per share, as per another filing, noting that he believes “it is essential for the Chief Executive Officer of any public company to purchase shares of such company in the open market with his or her own personal funds in order to further strengthen alignment with stockholders,” adding that any CEO who does not do the same “should be fired.”

The company’s Director Alain Attal also bought 12,000 shares on Jan. 21, at an average price of $21.63 and another director, Lawrence Cheng, acquired 5,000 shares of the company on Jan. 23, 2026, at an average price of $22.87 per share.

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around GME shares remained in the ‘extremely bullish’ territory over the past 24 hours amid ‘extremely high’ message volumes.

One bullish user said they would hold till the stock rallied to $100. GME shares are trading at around $24.78 levels at the time of writing.

https://stocktwits.com/Paulauska/message/642911456

Shares of GME have declined by over 9.5% in the past year.

data.title: Tradr to Add Three New ETFs
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Tradr ETFs plans to launch three new single-stock leveraged ETFs on Tuesday.

The ETF provider said Monday the new funds will be listed on Cboe. All three represent first-to-market strategies.

The new ETFs are for Lumentum, Sandisk and Western Digital. Each new ETF aims to deliver twice the daily performance of its specific underlying stock, Tradr said.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Texas's Tarrant County to Sell $186.9 Million of Bonds for Methodist Health System
data.description:
By Zaeem Shoaib

Texas' Tarrant County Cultural Education Facilities Finance Corp. plans to sell $186.9 million of bonds, with the proceeds going to Methodist Health System, which will use the money to finance and reimburse costs for two projects.

The issuance consists of Series 2026 Hospital Revenue Bonds, due Oct. 1 from 2026 through 2045, according to documents posted Friday on MuniOS. The bonds are expected to price on Feb. 4, with closing scheduled for Feb. 18.

Interest on the bonds will be payable semiannually on each April 1 and Oct. 1.

The bonds are payable solely from revenue derived by the corporation under a loan agreement with Methodist Health.

Proceeds from the issuance will be used to reimburse Methodist Health for the cost of two projects, including the construction of a new hospital in Celina, Texas, named Methodist Celina, and the improvements made at Methodist Richardson.

Dallas-based Methodist Health is a faith-based organization and operates 13 hospitals through ownership and affiliation along with more than 75 primary care and specialty clinics. It had operating revenue of $2.78 billion for the year-ended Sept. 30.

The Tarrant County Cultural Education Facilities Finance Corp. provides, expands and improves health facilities for bettering the adequacy, cost and accessibility of healthcare, research and education in the state.

Moody's has assigned a rating of Aa3 and S&P Global Ratings has assigned a rating of AA- to the bonds.

Hilltop Securities is lead manager on the issuance.

Write to Zaeem Shoaib at zaeem.shoaib@wsj.com

data.title: PREVIEW: UnitedHealth shares slide ahead of Q4 earnings
data.description:
** UnitedHealth Group shares tumbling 1.9% on Monday ahead of Q4 earnings due before the opening bell on Tues

** The health insurer is expected to post EPS of $2.10, a y/y decrease of 69.2% on revenue of $113.82 bln vs. $100.81 bln in Q4 2024, according to LSEG data

** UNH has met or beaten EPS estimate in six of past eight quarters, per LSEG

** Of the 30 analysts that cover UnitedHealth Group Inc, 20 have "strong buy" or "buy" ratings, 8 recommend "hold" and remaining 2 rate stock "sell" and "strong sell," (LSEG)

** Median price target of $410.00 is up 3.1% from beginning of Q4

** Including Mon's move, UNH shares up nearly 6% YTD vs ~3% rise in Dow Industrials over same time frame

** UNH options imply an 5.8% swing for the shares, in either direction by Friday, per Trade Alert data; that is lower than the stock's avg move of 7.4%, on the day of results over the last eight qtrs, per Trade Alert

data.title: NYC Snowfall Sparks Record Prediction-Market Trading — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nick Devor

The snowstorm that hit New York City this weekend was the largest in years. It was also a rare opportunity for prediction-market traders.

Leading prediction-market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket each had markets predicting how much snow New York will get from Saturday to Monday. As sleds descended the hills of Central Park, traders hunting for an edge ventured outside to take measurements of snowfall accumulation on parked cars.

Kalshi's NYC snowstorm market is the largest climate-related market the platform has ever seen, a company spokesperson told Barron's, with 17,418 traders having traded $5.1 million on the market as of early Monday afternoon.

That high level of participation and an unpredictable storm system led to a volatile market with aggressive buying and selling, making things difficult for even so-called "weathersharps" — traders who specialize in prediction markets related to the climate.

Some $433 million has been traded on Kalshi's climate markets, 1.3% of the platform's total trading volume, according to data-aggregator Dune. Most climate markets see less than $200,000 of trading and don't have anything to do with snow. The typical weather-related market on Kalshi or Polymarket predicts the highest or lowest temperature in a city on any given day. (Polymarket has a data partnership with Dow Jones, the publisher of Barron's.)

"It's rare we get a non-temp market with so much attention and liquidity like this," says a 31-year-old New York-based Kalshi trader whose username is NelsonOrAri. He started with a $100 deposit on the platform and has since made more than $70,000 trading climate events, putting him in the top 30 most-profitable traders in the category. His biggest single win on the weather markets came from correctly predicting the high temperature on December 17 in Los Angeles: 74 to 75 degrees.

The daily temperature markets give weather traders "a lot of opportunity for practice and understanding the possibilities in those markets," NelsonOrAri says. Markets based on accumulation are a different animal.

"There's no perfect way to track how much snow we're getting in the moment," the trader says, so trading activity on the weekend's snowstorm market was exceptionally dependent on readouts from the National Weather Service, leading to wild swings in prices.

By late afternoon Sunday, snowfall had shifted to a mix of sleet and snow. So when a surprise reading from the National Weather Service announced that accumulation had officially crossed 10 inches in Central Park, Kalshi's forecast spiked 20%, pricing in nearly 13 inches of snowfall. The snow had mostly stopped coming down by Monday afternoon and the market settled at a prediction of just over 11 inches.

While hedge funds and advanced meteorologists develop their own weather models to predict temperature and accumulation, many prediction-market traders lean on public data issued by the National Weather Service and interpretations from local forecasters.

"Not many trading areas have a whole industry full of people giving us guidance for free, so might as well take advantage of that," NelsonOrAri says.

Prediction-market traders often specialize in one kind of market, such as politics or economics, for fear of sharp traders in other markets with a more honed trading edge. One Kalshi trader who goes by Mark says he typically stays away from weather markets, but he told Barron's around noon Sunday that he was betting on more than a foot of snow.

Since he lives in the Astoria neighborhood of Queens, New York, he has one advantage that the weathersharps may not: "I can just go to my window and see the latest data."

Write to Nick Devor at nicholas.devor@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: IONQ: IonQ Stock Plunges on Billion-Dollar SkyWater Acquisition
data.description:
IONQ said Monday it will buy SkyWater Technology Corp. for $35.00 per share in a cash-and-stock deal valuing the target at $1.8 billion.

The agreement gives SkyWater holders $15.00 cash and $20.00 of IonQ common stock per share, subject to a collar that would leave SkyWater owners with roughly 4.4% to 6.7% of the combined company at closing. The $35 price is about a 38% premium to SkyWater's 30-day VWAP as of Jan. 23.

IonQ shares rose about 4% on the news. SkyWater (SKYT) jumped sharply on the premium.

Company executives said the tie-up would make IonQ a vertically integrated, full-stack quantum platform with a trusted U.S. foundry, and could accelerate functional testing of large QPUs toward a 2028 timetable, potentially enabling thousands of logical qubits.

The transaction is expected to close in the second or third quarter of 2026, subject to approvals.

IonQ also said it expects full-year 2025 revenue at the high end or above its prior $106 million to $110 million range.

data.title: Eaton to spin off lagging automotive units to focus on electrical and aerospace
data.description:
Key points:
  • Eaton's automotive units seen as growth drag by analysts
  • Eaton's focus shifts to electrical, aerospace amid geopolitical pressures
  • Eaton's Mobility Group expected $3.2 billion revenue by 2026
  • JPMorgan values divested units at up to $4 billion

By Sabrina Valle

Power management company Eaton Corp said on Monday it plans to spin off its underperforming automotive businesses to focus on more profitable AI‑driven power systems, aerospace and defense businesses.

Eaton’s Vehicle and eMobility operations have sat apart from a portfolio that has shifted toward power management and aerospace, and analysts have viewed the businesses' underperformance as a drag on the company’s growth. Shares were up 1.7% in afternoon trading.

"This may be as good a time as any for a cleanse," JPMorgan analyst Stephen Tusa said in a note after the news was first reported. "In the end, this type of transaction was inevitable."

JPM estimates the business could be worth up to roughly $4 billion if rivals decide to buy the operations.

The decision reflects broader geopolitical and industrial pressures that made the company buy bigger assets in its core areas.

The Dublin-based company has been pushing deeper into electrical equipment for data centers and power infrastructure, as well as aerospace systems tied to commercial aviation and defense.

It has since November announced plans to buy Boyd Thermal for $9.5 billion and Ultra PCS for $1.55 billion.

Eaton is investing into power grids, data centers and domestic manufacturing as geopolitical tensions, trade restrictions and supply‑chain disruptions push countries and companies to secure critical infrastructure and reshore production.

Those trends are driving demand for electrical equipment and aerospace systems tied to defense and commercial aircraft, while exposing the risks in more cyclical automotive supply chains.

The vehicle and eMobility businesses are expected to generate about $3.2 billion in revenue in 2026, with operating margins of roughly 13%, well below Eaton’s electrical and aerospace units, Jefferies analyst Stephen Volkmann said in a note.

The decision follows earlier portfolio moves by Eaton, which sold its lighting business in 2020 and its hydraulics unit in 2021 as it pared back slower‑growing segments.

Eaton expects to complete the transaction by the end of the first quarter of 2027.

The two businesses, together known as Eaton's Mobility Group, make technologies that manage and distribute power across vehicle propulsion systems.

Products include transmissions and clutches for commercial trucks and high-voltage components for electric vehicles, including fuses and valve control systems.

data.title: BOK Financial dips after D.A. Davidson downgrades to 'neutral'
data.description:
** Regional lender BOK Financial's shares fall 1.5% to $130.18 after D.A. Davidson downgrades stock rating to "neutral" from "buy" on valuation

** While BOKF is still well positioned for strong revenue growth, the positive outlook is already baked into the "well-deserved" premium valuation, D.A. Davidson analyst Peter Winter says

** Two of 10 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher and eight "hold"; median PT $135 — data compiled by LSEG

** BOKF stock rose 11.3% in 2025
